description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. If you create a traffic stream between connected hosts and enable the ECN bit for the hosts and start the traffic, a traffic capture may show that ECN bits arent copied from the inner header to the outer header.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?

Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. If you create a traffic stream between connected hosts and enable the ECN bit for the hosts and start the traffic, a traffic capture may show that ECN bits arent copied from the inner header to the outer header.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

description: In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer. For example: Copy !

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer. For example: Copy !

description: Symptom: The webserver becomes unresponsive, but AFC is reachable. Restarting HTTPS and Nginx services does not recover connectivity. Netstat for ports 443 and 80 do not work as expected. Scenario: In some scenarios where there is an excessive memory usage and resource exhaustion, the nginx process is incorrectly terminated by the switch leading to webservice to become unresponsive.
symptom: The webserver becomes unresponsive, but AFC is reachable. Restarting HTTPS and Nginx services does not recover connectivity. Netstat for ports 443 and 80 do not work as expected.
scenario: s where there is an excessive memory usage and resource exhaustion, the nginx process is incorrectly terminated by the switch leading to webservice to become unresponsive.

description: Symptom: Unexpected multicast flooding on VLAN after upgrading from 10.10 to 10.13. Scenario: In some scenarios when upgrading from 10.10 to 10.13, IGMP flooding can be observed for VLANs that have IP IGMP snooping enabled. Workaround: Disable and re-enable IP IGMP snooping on affected VLAN.

description: Symptom: Intermittent MSTP/RPVST starvation messages observed coming from source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00. Scenario: When 3rd party legacy devices send packets with source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00 to CX devices intermittent BPDU starvation might be observed. Workaround: Configure ACL to drop invalid source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00 in all switches where end devices are connected.

description: Symptom: Packets near MTU limits in size may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a client configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to max MTU are incorrectly dropped and not mirrored. This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.
symptom: Packets near MTU limits in size may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror.
scenario: , the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.
workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 342437?

Mirroring (Bug ID 342437): Symptom: Packets near MTU limits in size may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a client configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to max MTU are incorrectly dropped and not mirrored. This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

description: Symptom: The switch doesnt learn the MAC of a client that is denied on a secure port even when its moved to a trusted/non-secure port. Scenario: Configure port-access on a port (MAC_Auth/Dot1x/port-security). Setup the client MAC that is trying to onboard such that the MAC is denied. For example: Either by failing authentication or due to max client limit. Move such a client to a non-secure port. Observe the MAC table that the MAC is not moved to the non-secure port.

description: Symptom: VXLAN packets with UDP source port 69 are dropped at the wireless gateway. Scenario: Occurs when stub switch sends VXLAN packets with well-known ports instead of RFC7438 recommended range. Workaround: Move user traffic from vxlan vlan to a non-vxlan vlan.

description: Symptom: Delay of ~30s observed when updating VLAN access config via snmpset and verifying via snmpget immediately after. Scenario: When snmpset changes one OID and snmpget reads another OID before the cache can result in not updated cache values for OID. Workaround: Use the CLI for VLAN access configuration instead of SNMP.

description: Symptom: NTP clients unable synchronize clock when attempting to authenticate from upstream switch.Scenario: Configure the switches as NTP conductor for multiple downstream switches with authentication enabled. Workaround: Remove and re-add the same trusted key once time is synced on the server.
symptom: NTP clients unable synchronize clock when attempting to authenticate from upstream switch
scenario: Configure the switches as NTP conductor for multiple downstream switches with authentication enabled.
workaround: Remove and re-add the same trusted key once time is synced on the server.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX.
scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features.
workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, under what scenario does IGMP Bug 341085 occur?

IGMP (Bug ID 341085): If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what is the workaround for IGMP Bug 341085?
IGMP (Bug ID 341085): The documented workaround is: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
